figures.caption: Table 1. Crystallographic data for single-crystals of Cs2.4Cr8Te14 and Rb2.4Cr8Te14.

Figure 2. (a) Photographs of obtained crystals of Rb2.4Cr8Te14, Cs2.4Cr8Te14, and CsCr5Te8 taken on millimeter-sized graph paper. (b,c) SEM, SE (Secondary electron) images of (b) Cs2.4Cr8Te14 and (c) Rb2.4Cr8Te14. Yellow frames indicate the frame of the respective image with increased magnification of ×50, ×1000, and ×3000 taken perpendicular to the crystal’s surface.

Figure 3. (a) Schematic comparison of the crystal structures of hollandite-like ACr5Te8 and delafossite-like ACrTe2 phases with A2.4Cr8Te14 compounds presented in this work. Arrows indicate the combination of the structural motives (filled tunnels and intercalated layers) found in A2.4Cr8Te14. Crystal structures of (b) Cs2.4Cr8Te14 and (c) Rb2.4Cr8Te14 . Cr atoms are depicted in blue, Te in orange, Cs in green, and Rb in purple. Atoms located at y = 0 are depicted dark and atoms at y = 1/2 are depicted in light colors. Dashed arrows represent extensions of the Cr2Te2 bridges and underline the difference in stackings of layers.
